en: She's gonna need some sort of lawyer.
You know, J. Mitchell is so burnt out right now.
ko: J. 미첼은 지금 너무 지쳐 있어요.

en: He is in desperate need of a break.
Bench warrants are a no-brainer.
And, I mean, come on, does Gloria really look like
the kind of woman who would run a brothel?
Yeah, I better go with her.
ko: 네, 그녀와 함께 가는 게 좋겠어요.

en: You should go. Yeah. Mm-hmm.
ko: 가는 게 좋겠어요. 네. 음, 긴장하지 마세요.

en: Don't be nervous, all right?
I'll explain your situation.
We'll have you out of here in no time.
30 days in county jail. Oh! What?
Ooh, she's tough.
Next, Anita Menchaca, petty theft. See?
ko: 다음은 아니타 멘차카, 경범죄 절도입니다. 보세요?

en: Mister, you're a lawyer. Help me, please.
Oh, I'm sorry. I'm her lawyer.
I will help her so this will go faster.
ko: 제가 그녀를 도와서 이 일이 빨리 진행되도록 할게요.

en: She reminds me of my abuela
with her headscarf and her petty theft charges.
ko: 머리에 스카프를 쓰고 경범죄로 기소된 모습이요.

en: Here, Your Honor.
I'm Mitchell Pritchett, attorney to Miss Menchaca. Menchaca.
ko: 저는 미첬 프리첫, 멘차카 양의 변호사입니다. 멘차카.

en: How are you?
Are you ready to enter a plea? Absolutely.
I see no reason why this matter
ko: 이 사건이 법원의 소중한 시간을 더 이상 빼앗아서는 안 된다고 생각합니다.

en: should take up any more of the court's valuable time.
ko: 법원의 소중한 시간을 더 이상 차지해서는 안 됩니다.

en: I'm not guilty, Your Honor.
Why?
Gonna need a minute.
Your Honor, it's been a pleasure to serve with you.
